top_signals.title: US Army tests autonomous boats during Philippine exercise
top_signals.summary: Autonomous boats helped escort a U.S. Army vessel during a military exercise, in a demonstration of how uncrewed assets are changing maritime operations.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Inflation expectations impacting rate path, bonds and global risk assets.

top_signals.title: Europe Heat Wave Strains Power Supply, Sends Prices Soaring
top_signals.summary: Europe’s electricity system is under mounting pressure from an intense heat wave, with extreme swings in power prices as soaring temperatures drive up demand and curb output.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: US Exceptionalism Nearing Expiration Date, HSBC’s Kettner Says
top_signals.summary: Max Kettner, chief multi-asset strategist at HSBC, says investors may want to be “perhaps really leaning a bit more into that European story,” as he sees an August expiration date on US exceptionalism. (Source: Bloomberg)

top_signals.title: China’s Teapot Refineries Cut Operations to Their Lowest Level Since 2017
top_signals.summary: The independent refiners in China, the so-called teapots, have slashed their refinery run rates to the lowest level since 2017, as high feedstock prices, weak domestic fuel consumption, and restricted exports have hit margins. Refinery runs at the refiners in the Shandong province slumped to just 50.5% last week, per data from consultancy JLC cited by Bloomberg. These run rates are even lower than in 2020, when the pandemic hit China’s fuel consumption. The current utilization rates are at the lowest level since August 2017, when the independent…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.
